ratings.plot: A guide leads two men through an area known as the Zone to find a room that grants wishes.

verdict.reason: Tarkovsky's sci-fi masterpiece about desire and faith demands the big screen treatment.

ratings.plot: Holly Martins (Joseph Cotten), a writer of pulp Westerns, arrives in a bombed-out, post-war Vienna at the invitation of his childhood friend Harry Lime (Orson Welles) only to find him dead. Martins develops the ultimate conspiracy theory after learning of a “third man” present at the time of Harry’s death, butting up against interference from British police officer Major Calloway (Trevor Howard), and falling head-over-heels for Harry’s grief-stricken lover Anna (Alida Valli).

verdict.reason: Carol Reed's noir masterpiece with Orson Welles and that zither score - pure cinema gold.

ratings.plot: In this atomic adaptation of Mickey Spillane’s novel, directed by Robert Aldrich, the good manners of the 1950s are blown to smithereens. Ralph Meeker stars as snarling private dick Mike Hammer, whose decision one dark, lonely night to pick up a hitchhiking woman sends him down some terrifying byways. Brazen and bleak, KISS ME DEADLY is a film noir masterwork as well as an essential piece of Cold War paranoia, and it features as nervy an ending as has ever been seen in American cinema.

verdict.reason: Aldrich's atomic age noir that basically invented the modern action movie - essential viewing on the big screen.

ratings.plot: New York City newspaper writer J.J. Hunsecker holds considerable sway over public opinion with his Broadway column, but one thing that he can't control is his younger sister, Susan, who is in a relationship with aspiring jazz guitarist Steve Dallas. Hunsecker strongly disapproves of the romance and recruits publicist Sidney Falco to find a way to split the couple, no matter how ruthless the method.

verdict.reason: Burt Lancaster and Tony Curtis in the nastiest, most quotable film about media manipulation ever made.

ratings.plot: While trying to secure a $1 million donation for his museum, a befuddled paleontologist is pursued by a flighty and often irritating heiress and her pet leopard, Baby.

verdict.reason: Critics are united — this one earns its runtime.

ratings.plot: The children of a once famous artist (Ian McKellen) hire a forger (Michaela Coel) to complete some unfinished, long ago abandoned canvases so they'll have an inheritance when he dies.

verdict.reason: Soderbergh directing Ian McKellen and Michaela Coel in an art forgery comedy - that's an easy yes.

ratings.plot: A detective investigating a man's death in the mountains meets the dead man's mysterious wife in the course of his dogged sleuthing.

verdict.reason: Park Chan-wook crafts a gorgeous slow-burn that's equal parts detective story and doomed romance.

ratings.plot: Two neighbors form a strong bond after both suspect extramarital activities of their spouses. However, they agree to keep their bond platonic so as not to commit similar wrongs.

verdict.reason: Wong Kar-wai's masterpiece about longing and restraint is pure cinema poetry.

ratings.plot: Private eye Philip Marlowe helps friend Terry Lennox out of a jam and is implicated in his wife Sylvia's murder. He also is hired by Eileen Wade to locate her dipsomaniac husband Roger, who frequently disappears when he wants to d...

verdict.reason: Altman deconstructs Raymond Chandler with Elliott Gould as a laid-back 70s Marlowe. Essential for noir fans and Altman completists.

ratings.plot: Between 1968 and 1983, a San Francisco cartoonist becomes an amateur detective obsessed with tracking down the Zodiac Killer, an unidentified individual who terrorizes Northern California with a killing spree.

verdict.reason: Fincher's methodical procedural about the hunt for San Francisco's serial killer is journalism thriller at its finest. Builds dread through obsessive detail.

ratings.plot: In a corrupt, greed-fueled world, a powerful alchemist leads a messianic character and seven materialistic figures to the Holy Mountain, where they hope to achieve enlightenment.

verdict.reason: Jodorowsky's psychedelic spiritual journey is cinema as acid trip. Not for everyone but unforgettable for the adventurous.

ratings.plot: Dortmunder and his pals plan to steal a huge diamond from a museum. But this turns out to be only the first time they have to steal it...

verdict.reason: Robert Redford leads a diamond heist that keeps going wrong in this witty caper comedy. Old-school entertainment done right.

ratings.plot: Jeff "The Dude" Lebowski, mistaken for a millionaire of the same name, seeks restitution for his ruined rug and enlists his bowling buddies to help get it.

verdict.reason: The Dude's bowling alley odyssey is the Coens' most quotable comedy. Still holds up big on the big screen.

ratings.plot: Witek runs after a train. Three variations follow on how such a seemingly banal incident could influence the rest of Witek's life.

verdict.reason: Kieslowski explores how one moment can split a life into three different paths in this philosophical masterpiece. Essential viewing for anyone who thinks about fate and choice.

ratings.plot: Two children journey the long road to Germany to find the man they believe to be their father.

verdict.reason: Two Greek children search for their father in Germany in Angelopoulos' poetic meditation on childhood and hope. Slow but deeply moving.

ratings.plot: Intended to address the crisis levels of pollution in postwar Japan, GODZILLA VS. HEDORAH finds the King of the Monsters fighting an alien life form that arrives on Earth and steadily grows by feeding on industrial waste. Director Yoshimitsu Banno infuses the film with equal parts ecological horror, humorous monster antics, and '60s psychedelia straight out of San Francisco, making for a truly unique – and divisive – entry in the series. (Janus Films)

verdict.reason: The weirdest Godzilla movie ever made, with psychedelic sequences and environmental messaging as the monster fights a pollution creature. Essential for kaiju fans who want something truly bizarre.

ratings.plot: Lorne Michaels, creator of Saturday Night Live (1975), offers unprecedented access to the man who built and sustained the institution for five decades.

verdict.reason: Documentary about Saturday Night Live creator Lorne Michaels with unprecedented access to the comedy institution builder. Essential for anyone interested in how TV comedy actually works.

movies.title: Detective Bureau 2-3: Go to Hell Bastards!

ratings.plot: Police detective Tajima, tasked with tracking down stolen firearms, turns an underworld grudge into a blood-bath.

verdict.reason: Seijun Suzuki turns a routine cop story into stylized yakuza chaos with his signature visual madness. Essential for anyone who likes their crime films bonkers.

ratings.plot: Upon seeing a gorgeous chair in a showroom, Camille (Juliette Lewis) realizes that she truly envies the life of this perfect piece of furniture. If only she could be someone’s favorite thing. When she and the chair swap forms, Camille learns that she is better liked as an inanimate object than she was as a person: her mother (Betty Buckley) finds her to be a better listener, and her best friends (Samantha Mathis and Robin Tunney) enjoy a newfound, friction-free rapport. As the chair, Camille is unable to speak or move, and winds up in the hands of Olivier (Mamoudou Athie), a minimalist bachelor who gradually becomes romantically fixated on his elegant new possession. Narrated by Melanie Griffith, this modern fable celebrates the messiness of female interiority with deadpan humor, surrealist flourishes, and interpretative dance numbers. An absurdist spin on the body-swap comedy, BY DESIGN is a bold new work from singular filmmaker Amanda Kramer.

verdict.reason: Woman switches bodies with a chair and discovers she's more liked as furniture in this absurdist body-swap comedy. Weird premise executed with deadpan humor and dance numbers.

movies.title: Hello Mary Lou: Prom Night II

ratings.plot: When Mary Lou's boyfriend lights her on fire at prom, her spirit is caught in a pirate trunk. Thirty years later, the trunk is opened and Mary Lou is resurrected as a body-possessing death machine with one thing on her mind: VIOLENCE!! HELLO MARY LOU: PROM NIGHT II conclusively proves that prom night doesn't have to be filled with SUV limos, unwanted acne, and broken dreams. It can also be filled with exploding Bibles, death via Apple IIe computer, and Michael Ironside. Beautifully photographed and perfectly paced, this underrated melding of CARRIE and A NIGHTMARE ON ELM STREET feels like an episode of Beverly Hills: 90210 as directed by Sam Raimi. It's that good. (Joseph A. Ziemba)

verdict.reason: Vengeful prom queen returns from the dead to possess teenagers in this underrated 80s horror gem. Perfectly paced supernatural slasher that knows exactly what it is.

ratings.plot: Two young girls experience awakening sexuality in the heated atmosphere of a Swiss girls' boarding school...

verdict.reason: Two girls discover their sexuality at a Swiss boarding school in Radley Metzger's artful 1960s drama. Beautifully shot exploration of young desire.

movies.title: Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas

ratings.plot: An oddball journalist and his psychopathic lawyer travel to Las Vegas for a series of psychedelic escapades.

verdict.reason: Gilliam's gonzo masterpiece is pure sensory overload that demands the big screen experience.

ratings.plot: An Interpol investigation uncovers a shunned biologist and his daughter, who are involved in an alien plot to use Mechagodzilla and Titanosaurus to defeat Godzilla and wipe out humanity.

verdict.reason: Classic kaiju finale from Honda deserves respect, even if it's not peak Godzilla.
Kieslowski shorts are essential viewing for any serious film lover.
Prismatic Ground programs are always thoughtful and closing night means they saved something special.

movies.title: The Hour of Liberation Has Arrived

verdict.reason: If it's screening at an art house, this rare political documentary earned its spot.

ratings.plot: The young daughter of a journalist disappears into the desert without a trace-eight years later, the broken family is shocked when she is returned to them, as what should be a joyful reunion turns into a living nightmare.

verdict.reason: Evil Dead Rise director tackles Universal monster with missing daughter returned as supernatural threat. Cronin knows how to build dread and deliver scares.

ratings.plot: When a young Māori woman is summoned from New Zealand to North Yorkshire, she uncovers her horrific colonial heritage and is compelled to confront and destroy the titled Englishman who devastated her family.

verdict.reason: A young Māori woman travels to Yorkshire to confront the Englishman whose colonial violence destroyed her family. Sounds like a fierce revenge story about historical trauma.

ratings.plot: Shrouded in secrecy! Encircled by intrigue! The Mystery Machine promises to deliver cinematic treasures that span genres, countries, and tastes... only for those brave enough to enter. All shall be revealed only after the credits begin. Screening on select Mondays with discounted admission.

verdict.reason: Secret movie screenings are always a gamble but that's half the fun. Discounted admission and genuine surprise make it worth the risk.

ratings.plot: Things get complicated for Wayne and Garth as they make the jump from cable access to primetime. Rob Lowe is a sleazy network exec bent on exploiting the boys and making a hollow, big-budget version of the show – and bent on stealing Wayne’s girlfriend, Cassandra (Tia Carrere – schwing!). Can Wayne and Garth save their show, save Cassandra, and most crucially, continue to party on? Directed with a razor-sharp eye by Penelope Spheeris (DECLINE OF WESTERN CIVILIZATION, SUBURBIA), her lived-in knowledge of the rock scene and array of punks that populate it make the SNL -to-big-screen jump more real.

verdict.reason: Wayne and Garth face off against sleazy network executives trying to commercialize their cable access show. It's free, it's funny, and Penelope Spheeris actually knows the rock world she's filming.

ratings.plot: With unprecedented access to the official archives and intimate recollections from the band, both current and past, IRON MAIDEN: BURNING AMBITION invites fans to experience one of the most iconic journeys in music history. Spanning five decades, this electrifying documentary charts the band’s rise from the pubs of East London to the world’s biggest stadiums. Featuring exclusive interviews with band members and contributors such as Javier Bardem, Lars Ulrich, and Chuck D, as well as all-new animated sequences of the band's legendary mascot, Eddie, the film offers a rare and intimate look at Iron Maiden’s uncompromising vision and unwavering connection with their truly global army of fans.

verdict.reason: Official documentary chronicling Iron Maiden's five-decade journey from East London pubs to stadium shows. Features rare archive footage and animated Eddie sequences for metalheads.

ratings.plot: FIGHT CLUB contains several sequences with flashing lights that may affect those who are susceptible to photosensitive epilepsy or have other photosensitivities. Experience FIGHT CLUB remastered in stunning 4K quality for a limited time starting April 22nd. Directed by David Fincher, this dark satire stars Brad Pitt and Edward Norton. Norton gives a riveting performance as a label-obsessed insomniac who befriends a scummy soap salesman (Pitt) and embarks on an existential crusade that takes them both to the brutal, raw heights of their manhood. Also starring Helena Bonham Carter as the woman who comes between them, FIGHT CLUB may leave you feeling bruised and bloodied…but also invigorated and alive.

verdict.reason: Fincher's dark satire about toxic masculinity and consumer culture in stunning 4K restoration. The Brad Pitt-Edward Norton chemistry hits different on the big screen.

ratings.plot: Starring record-breaking comedian Nate Bargatze in his first feature film, THE BREADWINNER is a family comedy that follows the misadventures of Nate Wilcox, whose wife Katie (Mandy Moore) lands a once-in-a-lifetime deal on Shark Tank that takes her on a prolonged business trip – and now the lifelong breadwinner has to fend for his family as a first-time stay-at-home dad. With three young daughters and little experience handling their day-to-day needs, Nate finds out quickly that running the household is the toughest gig he’s ever had.

verdict.reason: Nate Bargatze's comedy chops don't translate to a generic stay-at-home dad premise we've seen a dozen times.

ratings.plot: In this witty, new breed of mystery, George (Hugh Jackman) is a shepherd who reads detective novels to his beloved sheep every night, assuming they can’t possibly understand. But when a mysterious incident disrupts life on the farm, the sheep realize they must become the detectives. As they follow the clues and investigate human suspects, they prove that even sheep can be brilliant crime-solvers.

verdict.reason: Hugh Jackman reading detective novels to sheep who become crime solvers sounds like family film committee nonsense. Even kids deserve better than this premise.

ratings.plot: A man becomes increasingly desperate when he realizes he is trapped in a subway station, needing to complete a mission to get out.

verdict.reason: Subway station trap premise sounds like a video game stretched too thin for 95 minutes.

ratings.plot: In a near-future, dystopian, "Free State of Florida," gun control is outlawed and school shootings are at an all-time high. When an opportunity for twisted notoriety arises, a tormented 13-year old, Sammy, enlists in a deadly comp...

verdict.reason: Dystopian Florida thriller about competitive school shootings sounds exploitative and tasteless. Hard pass.

ratings.plot: Sometimes the wrong place is right where you need to be. From Will Packer, the blockbuster producer of GIRL'S TRIP, the RIDE ALONG franchise, and ten movies that have opened No. 1 at the U.S. box office – including NIGHT SCHOOL, THIS CHRISTMAS, and THINK LIKE A MAN – comes a delicious romantic comedy about one little lie, one large Italian villa, and two people getting lost in the sauce of love. Halle Bailey (THE LITTLE MERMAID (2023), THE COLOR PURPLE (2023)) stars as Anna, a young woman who has abandoned her dreams of becoming a chef and is now drifting through her twenties with a series of bad choices. When Anna loses her house-sitting job (and housing) in one fell swoop, a chance encounter with Matteo – a handsome Italian who happens to have a villa sitting empty in Tuscany – will inspire her to jet off for Italy, against the advice of her always-honest bestie, Claire ( Home Before Dark ’s Aziza Scott). But Anna’s plan to crash at Matteo’s villa, without permission, just for one night, falls apart when Matteo’s mother, Gabriella (Italian film icon Isabella Ferrari), shows up at the house unexpectedly. In a panic, Anna allows Gabriella to believe that she is Matteo’s fiancée. That little lie becomes a big problem, though, when Matteo’s cousin, Michael (Regé-Jean Page; Bridgerton , BLACK BAG), shows up, and Anna discovers that the heat between them may ignite a fire that will transform her life.

verdict.reason: Woman lies about being engaged to crash at an Italian villa in this paint-by-numbers romantic comedy. Even the gorgeous setting can't save formulaic plotting.
42% RT score and we already got the Wachowskis version that flopped spectacularly.
33% RT suggests this revenge flick is generic Bollywood filler.

movies.title: PRIVATE EVENT TODAY IN THEATER & COMMISSARY

verdict.reason: Unless you're invited, this ain't for you.

ratings.plot: In Paris, a jaded writer is introduced by an old flame to her younger companion, and is subsequently drawn into a sadomasochistic relationship with the two women.

verdict.reason: Another Metzger adult film with mediocre scores and a plot that sounds dated.

ratings.plot: Two misfit best friends, Dar and Tuck, leave their dying coal-mining town with only one goal in mind - to reach sunny California and hook up with some beach babes. On the road, they meet gun-crazy outlaw Annie, and she takes over.

verdict.reason: 2.6 Letterboxd average for this road comedy tells you it's bottom-shelf 80s junk.

ratings.plot: The madame of a high-class new York brothel teaches her younger sister all about the business.

verdict.reason: Metzger's brothel drama might be classy for '70s adult fare but that 3.1 Letterboxd says it all.

ratings.plot: Have you ever seen hell? Because your about to be in it.

verdict.reason: A 63-minute horror film with that plot description and typo-ridden synopsis screams low-budget amateur hour. The internet meme deserves better.

movies.title: Mortal Kombat II HDR by Barco

ratings.plot: From New Line Cinema comes the latest high-stakes installment in the blockbuster video game franchise in all its brutal glory, MORTAL KOMBAT II. This time, the fan favorite champions – now joined by Johnny Cage himself – are pitted against one another in the ultimate, no-holds-barred, gory battle to defeat the dark rule of Shao Kahn that threatens the very existence of the Earthrealm and its defenders.

verdict.reason: The video game fighters face off against Shao Kahn in maximum brutality mode. More of the same CGI gore-fest without the novelty of the first reboot.

ratings.plot: In this musical, college classmates Todd Armstrong (Frankie Avalon) and Craig Gamble (Dwayne Hickman) head out on a group ski vacation, and agree that if they're going to get girlfriends, they need to understand how women think.

verdict.reason: 1960s beach party movie transplanted to the slopes with Frankie Avalon trying to understand women. Dated comedy that wasn't that funny to begin with.

ratings.plot: The former famous painter Frenhofer revisits an abandoned project using the girlfriend of a young visiting artist. Questions about truth, life, and artistic limits are explored.
Amid glittering casino lights and desert landscapes of Las Vegas, a blackjack dealer called Firdaus ponders the fate of her missing husband while her next-door neighbors deal in mutual abuse.
Utamaro is a great artist who lives to create portraits of beautiful women, using the brothels of Tokyo to provide his models.
A widow tries to marry off her daughter with the help of her late husband's three friends.
A spell of time of a rural family's slightly surreal life.
Centers on a temporary small-town sheriff who uncovers dark mysteries after a local bank robbery.

verdict.reason: Ben Wheatley doing small-town noir could be great but no audience scores yet make this a wild card.

ratings.plot: Presented in Japanese with English subtitles Long unavailable in the US, director Toshio Matsumoto’s shattering, kaleidoscopic masterpiece is one of the most subversive and intoxicating films of the late 1960s: a headlong dive into a dazzling, unseen Tokyo night world of drag queen bars and fabulous divas, fueled by booze, drugs, fuzz guitars, performance art, and black mascara. No less than Stanley Kubrick cited the film as a direct influence on his own dystopian classic A CLOCKWORK ORANGE. An unknown club dancer at the time, transgender actor Peter (from Kurosawa’s RAN) gives an astonishing Edie Sedgwick/Warhol superstar-like performance as hot young thing Eddie, hostess at Bar Genet – where she’s ignited a violent love triangle with reigning drag queen Leda (Osamu Ogasawara) for the attention of club owner Gonda (played by Kurosawa regular Yoshio Tsuchiya, from SEVEN SAMURAI and YOJIMBO). One of Japan’s leading experimental filmmakers, Matsumoto bends and distorts time here like Resnais in LAST YEAR AT MARIENBAD, freely mixing documentary interviews, Brechtian film-within-a-film asides, Oedipal premonitions of disaster, his own avant-garde shorts, and even on-screen cartoon balloons, into a dizzying whirl of image + sound. Featuring breathtaking black-and-white cinematography by Tatsuo Suzuki that rivals the photographs of Robert Mapplethorpe, FUNERAL PARADE OF ROSES offers a frank, openly erotic, and unapologetic portrait of an underground community of drag queens. Whether laughing with drunken businessmen, eating ice cream with her girlfriends, or fighting in the streets with a local girl gang, Peter’s ravishing Eddie is something to behold. “She has bad manners, all she knows is coquetry,” complains her rival Leda – but in fact, Eddie’s bad manners are simply being too gorgeous for this world. Her stunning presence in bell-bottom pants, black leather jacket, and Brian Jones hairdo, is a direct threat to the social order, both in the Bar Genet and in the streets of Tokyo. A key work of Japanese New Wave and queer cinema, FUNERAL PARADE has been beautifully restored in 4k from the original 35mm camera negative and sound elements.
A private detective hired to expose an adulterer in 1930s Los Angeles finds himself caught up in a web of deceit, corruption, and murder.

Jacques Tati’s gloriously choreographed, nearly wordless comedies about confusion in an age of high technology reached their apotheosis with PLAYTIME. For this monumental achievement, a nearly three-year-long bank-breaking production, Tati again thrust the lovably old-fashioned Monsieur Hulot, along with a host of other lost souls, into a baffling modern world, this time, Paris. With every inch of its super-wide frame crammed with hilarity and inventiveness, PLAYTIME is a lasting record of a modern era tiptoeing on the edge of oblivion. DCP courtesy of Janus Films

movies.title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire

ratings.plot: On an isolated island in Brittany at the end of the eighteenth century, a female painter is obliged to paint a wedding portrait of a young woman.
After being fired, a man devises a unique plan to secure a new job: eliminate his competition.
John Davidson: diagnosed with Tourette's syndrome at a young age which alienated him from his peers, he struggled with a condition few people had witnessed.

verdict.reason: Tourette's biography with strong IMDB and Letterboxd scores but that Metacritic gap suggests uneven execution.

movies.title: Strange Journey: The Story of Rocky Horror

ratings.plot: A London theater play evolves into a groundbreaking cult phenomenon, featuring iconic songs and performances that celebrate individuality. The legacy lives on through midnight screenings and a devoted following that spans generations.

An unprecedented look into the Loyalist community of the Craigyhill Estate in Larne, Northern Ireland as their master builders work toward achieving a world record by constructing the tallest bonfire in history.

verdict.reason: Northern Ireland bonfire documentary that critics love but audiences find less engaging.

ratings.plot: The life, times and afflictions of the fifteenth-century Russian iconographer St. Andrei Rublev.

After breaking the mysterious “One Wish Willow” to win his crush’s heart, a hopeless romantic finds himself getting exactly what he asked for, but soon discovers that some desires come at a dark, sinister price. Directed by Curry Barker. Starring Michael Johnston, Inde Navarrette, Cooper Tomlinson, Megan Lawless, and Andy Richter.
A robot who is responsible for cleaning a waste-covered Earth meets another robot and falls in love with her. Together, they set out on a journey that will alter the fate of mankind.

Meet a mother and daughter, high-society dropouts, reclusive cousins of Jackie O., managing to thrive together amid the decay and disorder of their East Hampton, NY, mansion, making for an eerily ramshackle echo of the American Ca...
ratings.director: Ellen Hovde, Albert Maysles, David Maysles

ratings.plot: Returning to a hotel now haunted by its mysterious past, an artist and her elderly mother confront long-buried secrets in their former family home.
After her werewolf lover unexpectedly dies in an accident, a young woman must find ways to raise their werewolf son and daughter while keeping their trait hidden from society.
A journey over the walls that divide us, to explore the age-old debate of land and power, amid the fight for greater access to nature.

verdict.reason: Title tells me nothing but sometimes the most mysterious films surprise.

ratings.plot: A science teacher wakes up alone on a spaceship. As his memory returns, he uncovers a mission to stop a mysterious substance killing Earth's sun and that an unexpected friendship may be the key.

verdict.reason: Lord and Miller directing sci-fi should work, but Andy Weir adaptations can get bogged down in exposition.

ratings.plot: In Boots Riley’s (SORRY TO BOTHER YOU) latest film, a crew of professional shoplifters take aim at a cutthroat fashion maven. It’s like community service. Starring Keke Palmer, LaKeith Stanfield, Demi Moore, and Don Cheadle.

movies.title: Star Wars: Episode IV – A New Hope

ratings.plot: A comedy (sort of) about young men in America.

verdict.reason: Vague premise about young men in America could be insightful or pretentious.

ratings.plot: Stemming from his 2012 record with The Coup and 2014 McSweeney's paperback, Boots Riley's 2018 feature debut has become more hilarious and incisive satires in subsequent years. In an alt-history Oakland, rent-seeking Cash (LaKeith Stanfield) stumbles into a telemarketer gig, only to find that his code-switching brilliance has put him on an express elevator to the top. Torn between class solidarity, the love of his radical artist girlfriend (Tessa Thompson), and his new Bay Area elite lifestyle, Cash finds himself on a journey through a capitalist dystopia that's both curious and strange. (Jake Isgar) DCP courtesy of Outsider Pictures
A recently-widowed woman is on the road with her precocious young son, determined to make a new life for herself as a singer.
A teenager in suburban Florida desperately hustles to make $5,000 in this dreamy and surreal animated coming-of-age story.

verdict.reason: Dreamy animated coming-of-age sounds promising but indie animation can be hit or miss.

ratings.plot: A group of international passengers en route from Los Angeles to Shanghai is forced to make an emergency landing in shark-infested waters. Now they must work together to overcome the frenzy of sharks drawn to the wreckage of their sinking plane.

FRANCES HA is a modern classic from director and writer Noah Baumbach (MARRIAGE STORY). This lovely film follows Frances, played by Greta Gerwig (director of LADY BIRD), a woman who doesn't exactly have it all together. With no real place to live and multiple rocky personal relationships, Frances tries to throw herself completely into the pursuit of her dream of becoming a modern dancer. Baumbach and Gerwig co-wrote this unapologetic look at the realities of pursuing what seem like unattainable dreams when the odds are against you. DCP courtesy of IFC Films
On a faraway planet where blue giants rule, oppressed humanoids rebel against their machine-like leaders.
After getting out of prison, small-time crook Mardar stumbles upon a woman who looks exactly like his long-lost lover.

Joyous, hilarious, and otherworldly are but a few ways to describe this liquid narrative from Leos Carax, creator of LOVERS ON THE BRIDGE, BOY MEETS GIRL, MAUVAIS SANG, and POLA X. In the performance(s) of a lifetime, regular Carax collaborator Denis Lavant is an amorphous actor on a chameleonic mission to inhabit a variety of public roles: a bag lady, an assassin, a sewer-dwelling freakazoid, a heated lover, and much more. Chauffeured around Paris by Edith Scob (EYES WITHOUT A FACE), Lavant commits to each character so thoroughly and engages you so quickly with each swap that it’s impossible to predict what comes next. Essential 21st-century viewing. (AGFA)

An ordinary factory worker buys a camera on the occasion of the birth of his child. The authorities order him to make documentaries about the factory's success. But his endeavor to be truthful leads him to opposition against censo...
Two boys meet at an opera training school in Peking in 1924. Their resulting friendship will span nearly 70 years and endure some of the most troublesome times in China's history.
An irresponsible and childish ex-con befriends a girl with cerebral palsy and develops a progressively stronger bond with her.

When novelist Ohm Bauman (Adam Scott) retreats to a remote inn to scatter his parents' ashes, he is consumed by tales of a witch haunting the honeymoon suite. Disturbing visions and a shocking disappearance force him to confront dark corners of his past. Director Damian McCarthy's previous film, ODDITY, thoroughly creeped us out. Expect jump scares, demented innkeepers, and psilocybin mushroom-spiked milk.

verdict.reason: Horror from the ODDITY director with Adam Scott sounds promising but no audience scores make this a blind date.

ratings.plot: In the enthralling BLOW OUT, brilliantly crafted by Brian De Palma (MISSION: IMPOSSIBLE), John Travolta (PULP FICTION) gives one of his greatest performances as a movie sound effects man who believes he has accidentally recorded a political assassination. He enlists the help of a possible eyewitness to the crime (Nancy Allen, ROBOCOP), who may be in danger herself, to uncover the truth. With its jolting stylistic flourishes, intricate plot, profoundly felt characterizations, and gritty evocation of early-1980s Philadelphia, BLOW OUT is an American paranoia thriller unlike any other, as well as a devilish reflection on moviemaking.
A 24-year-old music critic (Barbie Ferreira) gets romantically involved with two members of the same indie band she is covering, set against Montreal's vibrant indie music scene in 2011.

verdict.reason: Music critic love triangle in Montreal's indie scene could capture that era's energy or feel nostalgic.

ratings.plot: Follows the key people at an investment bank over a 24-hour period during the early stages of the 2008 financial crisis.

Before Marvel was sold to Disney and started hatching their methodical, annual, interlacing, box office bursting franchises, the studio tested out one of its lesser known anti-heroes. The first of the modern Marvel era found a star in the brooding half-vampire, half-mortal man. Wielding cool tech and Wesley Snipes's signature martial arts skills the film soon found a devout following and tapped into the popular culture of comics and vampires before they even knew they were cool. (Aaron Prescott)
John Wick is a former hitman grieving the loss of his true love. When his home is broken into, robbed, and his dog killed, he is forced to return to action to exact revenge.
Following a man's suicide, time traverses back to reveal six chapters of his life on why he committed suicide.

When a political decision is made as to the location of a new large chemical factory, Stefan Bednarz is put in charge of it. This honest communist party man has to confront the local community opposing the construction.
Forced to leave his family in Sicily, Mimí finds a job in a factory in Turin, where he gets involved in an extramarital affair.
The combustible chemistry between a Polish florist and a British tourist in this charming postcard of sapphic synchronicity.

verdict.reason: Polish-British lesbian romance sounds charming but slight at 71 minutes. Probably sweet but forgettable.

ratings.plot: Three lonely young denizens of Taipei unknowingly share an apartment used for sexual trysts.

Brian De Palma ascended to the highest ranks of American suspense filmmaking with this virtuoso, explicit erotic thriller. At once tongue-in-cheek and scary as hell, DRESSED TO KILL revolves around the grisly murder of a woman in Manhattan and how her psychiatrist, her brainiac teenage son, and the prostitute who witnessed the crime try to piece together what happened while the killer remains at large. With its masterfully executed scenes of horror, voluptuous camera work, and passionate score, DRESSED TO KILL is a veritable symphony of terror, enhanced by vivid performances by Angie Dickinson, Michael Caine, and Nancy Allen. DCP courtesy of Park Circus
On a dark, wet night a historic and regal Chinese cinema sees its final film. Together with a small handful of souls they bid "Goodbye, Dragon Inn".

movies.title: Mishima: A Life in Four Chapters

ratings.plot: Paul Schrader's visually stunning, collage-like portrait of acclaimed Japanese author and playwright Yukio Mishima (played by Ken Ogata) investigates the inner turmoil and contradictions of a man who attempted an impossible harmony between self, art, and society. Taking place on Mishima's last day, when he famously committed public seppuku, the film is punctuated by extended flashbacks to the writer's life, as well as by gloriously stylized evocations of his fictional works. With its rich cinematography by John Bailey, exquisite sets and costumes by Eiko Ishioka, and unforgettable, highly influential score by Philip Glass, MISHIMA: A LIFE IN FOUR CHAPTERS is a tribute to its subject and a bold, investigative work of art in its own right. (Janus)

Jake Scully (played with benign creepiness by Craig Wasson) is a wannabe actor who can’t even keep a cheap horror movie gig due to his paralyzing claustrophobia. Sleeping on a friend’s floor after catching his girlfriend aglow in climax with another, he finds a lucky turn when fellow struggling actor Sam offers him a place (and not just any dump – the legendary octagon-shaped Chemosphere, once parodied in The Simpsons as Troy McClure’s residence). Even better, it comes with a view – a peep through a telescope lands eyes on a neighbor who does a nightly dance in the nude. Jake can’t help but look, and then he realizes he’s not the only one looking. Soon he comes to witness the woman’s brutal murder, not only unable to stop it, but also becoming a suspect himself. A chance viewing of a late-night TV spot with porn star Holly Body (Melanie Griffith with one of the best looks ever) puts Jake on a path to figure out what really happened – he thinks Holly can help him, if he can convince her. Brian De Palma takes bits of REAR WINDOW and VERTIGO and sleazes them up ‘80s style in a movie that has dark humor, thrills, and sexiness that all add up to magnificent pleasure.
After hitting something with her car, a bourgeois Argentine woman's life slowly descends into paranoia and isolation, as she fears she may have killed someone.
A happily-engaged couple is put to the test when an unexpected turn sends their wedding week off the rails.

verdict.reason: A couple's pre-wedding drama unfolds when secrets surface days before the ceremony. Sounds like standard rom-com territory but could surprise.

ratings.plot: A divorced woman and her diabetic daughter take refuge in their newly-purchased house's safe room when three men break-in, searching for a missing fortune.

Follows Miranda Priestly's struggle against Emily Charlton, her former assistant turned rival executive, as they compete for advertising revenue amidst declining print media while Miranda nears retirement.

verdict.reason: Miranda Priestly battles her former assistant Emily in the declining print media world. Has potential with the original cast but late sequels are usually cash grabs.

ratings.plot: After his wife leaves, James quits his US career to care for his ailing mother with memory loss in his homeland. Their relationship grows hostile as he becomes her caregiver, leading to a journey of emotional healing.

verdict.reason: A man returns home to care for his mother with memory loss, leading to family tensions and healing. Feels like familiar indie drama territory.

ratings.plot: It sounds like a great night, right? Double date, carnival...what could go wrong? Well, as you might imagine, plenty. First of all, did we mention that this is a mysterious carnival? As if that wasn't enough, the double duo decides to spend the night there. If this was a comedy, hijinks would ensue. But it's a slasher film. The real question is, do you dare to join us for a night of early-80s horror movie danger?
The magical inhabitants of a rainforest fight to save their home, which is threatened by logging and a polluting force of destruction called Hexxus.

verdict.reason: Environmental fairy tale about magical forest creatures fighting deforestation hits different in 2024. Nostalgic for millennials, preachy for everyone else.

ratings.plot: Brian De Palma (SCARFACE, THE UNTOUCHABLES) directs this edge-of-your-seat thrill ride that ignited one of the most spectacular action film franchises of all time. Tom Cruise stars as Ethan Hunt, a secret agent framed for the deaths of his espionage team. Fleeing from government assassins, breaking into the CIA's most impenetrable vault, clinging to the roof of a speeding bullet train, Hunt races to stay one step ahead of his pursuers and draw one step closer to discovering the shocking truth. Your mission, should you choose to accept it: don't miss this rare opportunity to see the one that started it all on the big screen!
A mother desperately searches for the killer who framed her son for a girl's horrific murder.

BLUE VELVET and PINK FLAMINGOS got together and had a '90s baby. After the family-friendly fare of HAIRSPRAY and CRY-BABY, John Waters swung the pendulum back to the dark side for this devilishly cuckoo take on the pressures of motherhood. This gift-that-keeps-on-giving wields the magic power of turning back the mental clock on any audience member, leaving nothing but the 12-year-old brat inside us all. No stranger to outré acting challenges (Ken Russell’s CRIMES OF PASSION, anyone?), Kathleen Turner here gives you 87,000 reasons why she’s such a great movie star – a big one being her ability to effortlessly sell the idea of a blood-lusted psychopath as a sympathetic figure. Let’s also not forget that SERIAL MOM has one of the best “video store” scenes in film history!

When miserable couple Dan and Lisa retreat to a remote cabin for a romantic reset, each arrives with a secret plan to murder the other. Their carefully plotted traps and counterplots quickly unravel when a dangerous crew crashes the weekend with plans of their own. As the toxic getaway spirals into chaotic carnage, Dan and Lisa must survive the intruders, each other, and figure out if they want to save their marriage or survive it.

verdict.reason: Couple planning to murder each other gets interrupted by dangerous intruders at their cabin retreat. Dark comedy premise has potential if the execution lands.
No plot details available for this title. Hard to judge without knowing what it's about.

ratings.plot: James Bond is sent to disrupt a media mogul's plot to foment a war between China and the United Kingdom.

verdict.reason: Bond fights a media mogul trying to start a war in this mid-tier Brosnan entry. Solid action but forgettable among the franchise.

ratings.plot: Believe it or not, there was a time when Blade, the gun-toting, sword-swinging, karate-kicking vampire hunter embodied by Wesley Snipes, was the most—in fact, the only—successful Marvel superhero in cinema. Guillermo del Toro may have seemed an odd choice for Blade II, especially after the critical success of his allegorical horror masterpiece The Devil’s Backbone, but screenwriter David S. Goyer was a fan of Del Toro’s Cronos, and the director was thrilled by the opportunity to make vampires scary again. This time, Blade teams up with a crack squad of vampire commandos—who, as it happens, were initially assembled to kill him—to loiter in dank Easter European nightclubs and hunt a vicious, highly virulent new strain of bloodsucker. The film may be thin on plot, but it’s heavy on blood, leather, and outstanding creature designs. (MOMA) DCP courtesy of Warner Bros.
Two psychopathic young men take a family hostage in their cabin.

Sissy Spacek makes Carrie a truly frail creature whose wide eyes are so much like a cat caught in the light that you’d rather keep your distance to avoid the scratch. Enduring the humiliation that is the true stuff of teenage girl nightmares – public shame for getting your period – Carrie is constantly on the receiving end of her classmates’ pranks. At home, she is taunted with religious nonsense and talk of dirty pillows, so what’s a girl to do but transfer all that rage into telekinetic powers? Building to the end that everyone knows even if you haven’t seen the movie (has someone out there really not seen it?), CARRIE is so effectively terrifying, repeat viewings won’t numb you to it.
Vice detectives Crockett and Tubbs, from Miami-Dade's organized crime bureau, go undercover as criminals to infiltrate and dismantle southern Florida's criminal enterprises.

verdict.reason: Kosinski knows how to shoot action but 47% suggests the story doesn't match the visuals.

ratings.plot: A canine angel, Charlie, sneaks back to earth from heaven but ends up befriending an orphan girl who can speak to animals. In the process, Charlie learns that friendship is the most heavenly gift of all.
ratings.director: Don Bluth, Gary Goldman, Dan Kuenster

ratings.plot: Mario ventures into space, exploring cosmic worlds and tackling galactic challenges far from the familiar Mushroom Kingdom.

verdict.reason: Kids will love the space setting but 44% means it's probably just corporate product.

ratings.plot: The story of the famous musician Michael Jackson, known as the King of Pop.

movies.title: How to Stuff a Wild Bikini

ratings.plot: Frankie, away from home on Naval Reserve duty in Tahiti, calls upon Bwana, a witch doctor, to keep his girl friend Dee Dee away from male competition. Bwana sends a pelican to watch over Dee Dee, but when handsome girl-chaser Ricky starts to court Dee Dee, Bwana decides to use more powerful magic. He sends a mysterious charmer, Cassandra, to distract Ricky. Peachy Keane, an advertising man, arrives at the beach with plans to find the "Girl Next Door" for an advertising campaign and enlists the aid of Eric Von Zipper and his gang, the Rat Pack, who are preparing for an upcoming motorcycle competition. Cassandra wins the "Girl Next Door" contest; but Peachy soon discovers that she is completely uncoordinated. In a motorcycle chase, Von Zipper and his gang meet with difficulties, and Von Zipper trades in his leather jacket for a grey flannel suit. Ricky dates Cassandra, and Frankie returns to Dee Dee. (American Film Institute)

Presented in French with English subtitles Pénélope can’t sleep. Startled awake by the screams of her new baby, she decides to take a late-night walk to calm him. She unwittingly walks into a crime scene, and witnessing the violence loosens the last screw in her unraveling mental health. As her insomnia intensifies, she’s plagued by visions, and without understanding or assistance from her boyfriend, her behavior becomes more and more erratic. Adding to her anxiety is the unresolved trauma from her sister’s death, and Pénélope's past begins to blend with her future into a confused, nightmare-like existence. As she seeks comfort in increasingly self-destructive ways, she goes further and further off the deep end, taking the audience along for the terrifying ride. A visually striking first feature from writer/director Chloé Cinq-Mars, the film is exquisitely crafted with great care put into every aspect, from the cinematography to the striking sound design. Pénélope’s rage and existential unraveling are fully believable thanks to a phenomenal performance by Rose-Marie Perreault as a woman battling postpartum demons. A tense psychological thriller that doesn’t skimp on extremely unsettling imagery, NESTING is an impactful, resonant film destined to stick with you long after viewing. (Lisa Dreyer)

movies.title: Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind

ratings.plot: A man is discovered digging a grave for his wife in the middle of the desert, and is arrested for her murder.

movies.title: The Mystery of Picasso + Rembrandt: Painter of Men
A Bigger Splash + Mark Turbyfill by Markopoulos

ratings.plot: "Alice, an overworked woman trapped between a demeaning boss and an uncaring husband, discovers a mysterious pill called Elysium that makes everything seem perfect. In this distorted wonderland, the boundaries between real and ima...

verdict.reason: Pill-popping Alice in Wonderland metaphor sounds obvious but shorts can surprise you.

movies.title: Aloïse preceded by Qui donc a rêvé?
Carrie Mae Weems selects No Country for Old Men
Hiroshi Sugimoto selects The Face of Another
Lee Friedlander selects North by Northwest
Muriel or the Time of Return
Nan Goldin selects The Naked Kiss

ratings.plot: A complex look at one of the greatest figures in 20th century classical music, Leonard Bernstein, whose passion and creativity guided him well beyond the concert hall.

verdict.reason: The shouting title makes me curious but could go either way.

ratings.plot: A black-and-white love letter to pre-gentrification New York City, Phil Hartman's NO PICNIC captures a remote time and place - the East Village circa 1985, a vibrant, seedy neighborhood populated by musicians, pimps and poets. Mac...

Two young boys from very different backgrounds become friends in 1933 Stuttgart. However, they don't realize how different they are until much later as one is the son of a well-to-do Jewish doctor and the other the son of a German...

While his latest movie ¿Quieres ser John Malkovich? (1999) is in production, screenwriter Charlie Kaufman is hired by Valerie Thomas to adapt Susan Orlean's non-fiction book The Orchid Thief for the screen. Thomas bought the movie rights before Orlean wrote the book, when it was only an article in The New Yorker . The book details the story of rare orchid hunter John Laroche, whose passion for orchids and horticulture made Orlean discover passion and beauty for the first time in her life. Charlie wants to be faithful to the book in his adaptation, but despite Laroche himself being an interesting character in his own right, Charlie is having difficulty finding enough material in Laroche to fill a movie, while equally not having enough to say cinematically about the beauty of orchids. At the same time, Charlie is going through other issues in his life. His insecurity as a person doesn't allow him to act upon his feelings for Amelia Kavan, who is interested in him as a man.

Presented in Korean and Japanese with English subtitles From master filmmaker Park Chan-wook (OLDBOY, STOKER, LADY VENGEANCE) comes a gripping and sensual tale of two women – a young Japanese Lady living on a secluded estate, and a Korean woman who is hired to serve as her new handmaiden, but is secretly plotting with a conman to defraud her of a large inheritance. Inspired by the novel Fingersmith by British author Sarah Waters, THE HANDMAIDEN borrows the most dynamic elements of its source material and combines it with Park’s singular vision to create an unforgettable viewing experience.

movies.title: Attack on Titan: THE LAST ATTACK (Subtitled)

ratings.plot: Presented in Japanese with English subtitles Back on the big screen for Crunchyroll Anime Nights in an awesome new 4k version! Eren’s apocalyptic rumbling begins, unleashing an unstoppable wave of Colossal Titans marching to crush the world. Having uncovered Paradis’ brutal history and the world’s hatred for Eldians, he commits to eliminating all threats. Now, former allies and enemies unite in desperation for one final mission – to stop him before nothing remains.

verdict.reason: The final anime film brings Eren's world-ending rampage to theaters in 4K. Fans of the series will want the big screen experience, newcomers will be lost.

movies.title: Billie Eilish - Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour

ratings.plot: Note: This film is also playing in 3D at select locations. Click here to find a screening near you. Captured during her sold-out world tour, BILLIE EILISH - HIT ME HARD AND SOFT: THE TOUR brings an innovative new concert experience to the big screen from one of the most celebrated and successful artists of her generation. The film is directed by Academy Award® winners James Cameron and Billie Eilish, in theaters May 8, 2026.

movies.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2 Costume Screening

ratings.plot: THE DEVIL WEARS PRADA 2 contains several sequences with flashing lights that may affect those who are susceptible to photosensitive epilepsy or have other photosensitivities. Twenty years after making their iconic turns as Miranda, Andy, Emily, and Nigel, Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt, and Stanley Tucci return to the fashionable streets of New York City and the sleek offices of Runway Magazine in the eagerly awaited sequel to the 2006 phenomenon that defined a generation.

verdict.reason: Twenty years later sequel with the original cast returning to fashion magazine world. Costume screenings add party atmosphere but sequels this late rarely recapture the magic.

movies.title: Mortal Kombat II Mother's Day Fan Screening

ratings.plot: From New Line Cinema comes the latest high-stakes installment in the blockbuster video game franchise in all its brutal glory, MORTAL KOMBAT II. This time, the fan favorite champions – now joined by Johnny Cage himself – are pitted against one another in the ultimate, no-holds-barred, gory battle to defeat the dark rule of Shao Kahn that threatens the very existence of the Earthrealm and its defenders.

The fan favorite champions -- now joined by Johnny Cage himself -- are pitted against one another in the ultimate battle to defeat the dark rule of Shao Kahn that threatens the very existence of the Earthrealm and its defenders.

verdict.reason: Video game sequel adds Johnny Cage to the roster for battles against Shao Kahn. The first one was decent dumb fun, this looks like more of the same.

ratings.plot: After breaking the mysterious “One Wish Willow” to win his crush’s heart, a hopeless romantic finds himself getting exactly what he asked for, but soon discovers that some desires come at a dark, sinister price. Directed by Curry Barker. Starring Michael Johnston, Inde Navarrette, Cooper Tomlinson, Megan Lawless, and Andy Richter.

After breaking the mysterious “One Wish Willow” to win his crush’s heart, a hopeless romantic finds himself getting exactly what he asked for, but soon discovers that some desires come at a dark, sinister price. Directed by Curry Barker. Starring Michael Johnston, Inde Navarrette, Cooper Tomlinson, Megan Lawless, and Andy Richter.

movies.title: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu

ratings.plot: STAR WARS: THE MANDALORIAN AND GROGU contains several sequences with flashing lights that may affect those who are susceptible to photosensitive epilepsy or have other photosensitivities. The evil Empire has fallen, and Imperial warlords remain scattered throughout the galaxy. As the fledgling New Republic works to protect everything the Rebellion fought for, they have enlisted the help of legendary Mandalorian bounty hunter Din Djarin (Pedro Pascal) and his young apprentice Grogu.

movies.title: I Love Boosters with Livestream Q&A

ratings.plot: In Boots Riley’s (SORRY TO BOTHER YOU) latest film, a crew of professional shoplifters take aim at a cutthroat fashion maven. It’s like community service. Starring Keke Palmer, LaKeith Stanfield, Demi Moore, and Don Cheadle.

IN THE GREY follows a covert team of elite operatives living in the global shadows. When a ruthless despot steals a billion-dollar fortune, they’re sent to take it back – an impossible heist that erupts into a deadly game of strategy, deception, and survival.

Acclaimed filmmaker Andrew Bujalski (SUPPORT THE GIRLS, COMPUTER CHESS) explores the precarious nature of human connection in THERE THERE, a round-robin of emotional two-character vignettes in which seven people reach out and bond momentarily, setting the scene for the next encounter. A pair of new lovers, an alcoholic and her AA sponsor, a teacher and a parent, a lawyer and his client, and more move from scene to scene, revealing intimate details as they forge connections. Via a groundbreaking shooting process, Bujalski plays with ideas of isolation and perception by filming each actor individually in separate locations, only joining them to their partner’s performance in the edit. An extraordinary cast including Jason Schwartzman, Lennie James, Lili Taylor, and Molly Gordon sensitively captures the humor and heartbreaking fragility of life and love in the 2020s

Director Robert Zemeckis' deliciously twisted black comedy begins with Broadway has-been Meryl Streep bulldozing her way through a memorably misguided disco number and Goldie Hawn gorging herself on canned cake frosting – and things only get weirder and wilder from there. DEATH BECOMES HER offers up a classic catfight unlike anything you've ever seen, as terminally vain Madeline Ashton (Streep) clashes with her lifelong rival, beauty expert Helen Sharp (Hawn), in a perilous quest for eternal youth and glamour. Caught in the middle are plastic surgeon Ernest Menville (Bruce Willis) and the mysterious goddess-y Lisle Von Rhuman (Isabella Rossellini), who peddles an ancient elixir that provides "a touch of magic in this world obsessed with science." We've often heard "beauty is pain," and Madeline and Helen take that old adage to hair-raising new extremes. DEATH BECOMES HER deservedly won the best visual effects Oscar in 1993. But the eye-popping digital wizardry doesn't eclipse sensational comic performances from Streep, Hawn, and Willis, who create a hilariously offbeat love triangle under Zemeckis' stylish direction, and Alan Silvestri's urgently over-the-top score is the perfect frosting on this bittersweet cake. Yes, these are the moments that make life worth living...

MAMMA MIA! is the ultimate feel-good show that has audiences coming back again and again to relive the thrill. Now it’s your turn to have the time of your life at this smash-hit musical that combines ABBA’s greatest hits, including "Dancing Queen," "S.O.S.," "Super Trouper," "Take A Chance on Me," and "The Winner Takes It All," with an enchanting tale of love, laughter, and friendship. Whether it’s your first visit or your fourteenth, see the show that has the whole world coming back for more, because every time feels like the first time at MAMMA MIA! In the 2008 film adaptation, Pierce Brosnan, Colin Firth, Stellan Skarsgård, Christine Baranski, Julie Walters, Amanda Seyfried, and Dominic Cooper join Meryl Streep in this celebration of a mother, a daughter, and three possible dads. An independent, single mother who owns a small hotel on an idyllic Greek island, Donna (Streep) is about to let go of Sophie (Seyfried), the spirited daughter she's raised alone. For Sophie's wedding, Donna has invited her two lifelong best girlfriends – practical and no-nonsense Rosie (Julie Walters) and wealthy, multi-divorcee Tanya (Christine Baranski ) – from her one-time backing band, Donna and the Dynamos. But Sophie has secretly invited three guests of her own. On a quest to find the identity of her father to walk her down the aisle, she brings back three men from Donna's past to the Mediterranean paradise they visited 20 years earlier. Over 24 chaotic, magical hours, new love will bloom and old romances will be rekindled on this lush island full of possibilities.

verdict.reason: Meryl Streep singing ABBA hits on a Greek island with Pierce Brosnan's questionable vocals. Movie parties are fun group experiences if you embrace the cheese.

ratings.plot: Winner of the first Academy Award for Best Animated Feature, SHREK sparked a motion picture phenomenon and captured the world's imagination with the Greatest Fairy Tale Never Told! Relive every moment of Shrek's daring quest to rescue the feisty Princess Fiona with the help of his loveable, loudmouthed Donkey and win back the deed to his beloved swamp.

verdict.reason: The DreamWorks fairy tale subversion that launched a franchise gets the party treatment. Still holds up but movie parties are for people who want to sing along.

ratings.plot: Winner of the first Academy Award for Best Animated Feature, SHREK sparked a motion picture phenomenon and captured the world's imagination with the Greatest Fairy Tale Never Told! Relive every moment of Shrek's daring quest to rescue the feisty Princess Fiona with the help of his loveable, loudmouthed Donkey and win back the deed to his beloved swamp.

verdict.reason: The original ogre adventure that won the first Best Animated Feature Oscar remains clever. Worth revisiting for the voice work and fairy tale satire.

movies.title: Power To The People : John & Yoko Live In NYC

ratings.plot: POWER TO THE PEOPLE: JOHN & YOKO LIVE IN NYC is the 2026 multiscreen concert film of two massive live shows by John Lennon & Yoko Ono at Madison Square Garden, New York City, on August 30, 1972, newly restored, re-edited, and remixed by the Lennons’ seven-times GRAMMY®-Award winning team. John & Yoko/Plastic Ono Band with Elephant's Memory and Special Guests performed these now-legendary sold-out One To One concerts to a combined audience of 40,000 people, raising over $1.5M (equivalent to $11.5M in 2026) for children with intellectual and developmental disabilities. They were the only full-length concerts John Lennon (with Yoko Ono) performed after leaving The Beatles. Originally filmed by multi-camera director Steve Gebhardt in 1972, this 2026 version of the concert film is directed by Simon Hilton, edited by Ben Wainwright-Pearce, and produced by Peter Worsley & Sean Ono Lennon. Music Production is by Sean Ono Lennon; mixed and engineered by Paul Hicks & Sam Gannon. Hits performed include John's "New York City," "Instant Karma!," "Imagine," and "Mother," plus Yoko's "Don't Worry Kyoko" and "Open Your Box," plus rousing renditions of "Come Together" and "Hound Dog," and an encore of "Give Peace a Chance" with special guests Stevie Wonder, Melanie, and many others.

RZA’S ONE SPOON OF CHOCOLATE follows Unique (Shameik Moore, DOPE and voice of Miles Morales in the Spider-Verse films), a veteran and ex-convict seeking a fresh start in a small town. After an altercation with a gang of locals, he starts to suspect they may have something to do with the disappearance of young men in the area – including his cousin. As he digs for the truth, he finds himself the target of not only the gang but the local sheriff’s office, whose involvement in the disappearances may be even more sinister. Instead of waiting for his turn to be picked off, Unique and those closest to him (Paris Jackson, RJ Cyler) decide to fight back in this sharply satirical and stylish action thriller from the visionary leader of the Wu-Tang Clan.

Margot Kidder is Danielle, a beautiful model separated from her Siamese twin, Dominique. When a hotshot reporter (Jennifer Salt) suspects Dominique of a brutal murder, she becomes dangerously ensnared in the sisters’ insidious sibling bond. A scary and stylish paean to female destructiveness, Brian De Palma’s first foray into horror voyeurism is a stunning amalgam of split-screen effects, bloody birthday cakes, and a chilling score by frequent Alfred Hitchcock collaborator Bernard Herrmann.

Danny Boyle's explosive film tracks the misadventures of young men in Edinburgh trying to find their way out of joblessness, aimless relationships, and drug addiction. Some are successful, while others hopelessly are not. Based on Irvine Walsh's novel, TRAINSPOTTING melds grit with poetry, resulting in a film of harsh truths and stunning grace.

movies.title: That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime the Movie: Tears of the Azure Sea (Dubbed)

ratings.plot: Presented in an English dub After concluding the opening ceremony of the Demon Kingdom Federation Tempest, Rimuru and his companions are invited by the Celestial Emperor Hermesia of the great elven nation – the Magi Dynasty Salion – to visit her private resort island. As the group enjoys their brief vacation, a mysterious woman named Yura appears. A new incident unfolds against the backdrop of the boundless azure sea.

movies.title: That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime the Movie: Tears of the Azure Sea (Subtitled)

ratings.plot: Presented in Japanese with English subtitles After concluding the opening ceremony of the Demon Kingdom Federation Tempest, Rimuru and his companions are invited by the Celestial Emperor Hermesia of the great elven nation – the Magi Dynasty Salion – to visit her private resort island. As the group enjoys their brief vacation, a mysterious woman named Yura appears. A new incident unfolds against the backdrop of the boundless azure sea.

Clint Eastwood's THE BRIDGES OF MADISON COUNTY is not about love and not about sex, but about an idea. The film opens with the information that two people once met and fell in love, but decided not to spend the rest of their lives together. The implication is: If they had acted on their desire, they would not have deserved such a love. Almost everybody knows the story by now. Robert James Waller's novel has been a huge best-seller. Its prose is not distinguished, but its story is compelling: he provides the fantasy of total eroticism within perfect virtue, elevating to a spiritual level the common fantasy in which a virile stranger materializes in the kitchen of a quiet housewife and takes her into his arms. (Roger Ebert)

Two titles are on the line when UFC returns to Prudential Center in Newark, New Jersey, on Saturday, May 9, for UFC 328. In the main event, undefeated champion Khamzat Chimaev defends the middleweight title against former titleholder Sean Strickland for the first time since winning it last August. In the co-main event, flyweight champion Joshua “The Fearless” Van defends his title against Japanese contender Tatsuro “The Best” Taira in the first UFC championship fight between competitors born in the 21st century.
Exploding out of the AGFA vaults as a brand-new 35mm preservation, this 1990s kung fu fantasia from Hong Kong is a breathtaking combination of action, comedy, and cooking.

movies.title: Billie Eilish - Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D)

ratings.plot: Captured during her sold-out world tour, BILLIE EILISH - HIT ME HARD AND SOFT: THE TOUR (LIVE IN 3D) brings an innovative new concert experience to the big screen from one of the most celebrated and successful artists of her generation. Presented in immersive 3D, the film is directed by Academy Award® winners James Cameron and Billie Eilish, in theaters May 8, 2026.

movies.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2 HDR by Barco

ratings.plot: THE DEVIL WEARS PRADA 2 contains several sequences with flashing lights that may affect those who are susceptible to photosensitive epilepsy or have other photosensitivities. Twenty years after making their iconic turns as Miranda, Andy, Emily, and Nigel, Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt, and Stanley Tucci return to the fashionable streets of New York City and the sleek offices of Runway Magazine in the eagerly awaited sequel to the 2006 phenomenon that defined a generation.

verdict.reason: Meryl Streep and the gang return to Runway Magazine twenty years later. Nostalgia bait that'll satisfy fans of the original but probably won't recapture the magic.

Directed by Curry Barker. Starring Michael Johnston, Inde Navarrette, Cooper Tomlinson, Megan Lawless, and Andy Richter.

movies.title: I Love Boosters with Live Q&A

ratings.plot: In Boots Riley’s (SORRY TO BOTHER YOU) latest film, a crew of professional shoplifters take aim at a cutthroat fashion maven. It’s like community service. Starring Keke Palmer, LaKeith Stanfield, Demi Moore, and Don Cheadle.

movies.title: I Love Boosters Fashion Forward Filanthropy Screening

ratings.plot: In Boots Riley’s (SORRY TO BOTHER YOU) latest film, a crew of professional shoplifters take aim at a cutthroat fashion maven. It’s like community service. Starring Keke Palmer, LaKeith Stanfield, Demi Moore, and Don Cheadle.

MICHAEL (2026) is the cinematic portrayal of the life and legacy of one of the most influential artists the world has ever known. The film tells the story of Michael Jackson’s life beyond the music, tracing his journey from the discovery of his extraordinary talent as the lead of the Jackson Five, to the visionary artist whose creative ambition fueled a relentless pursuit to become the biggest entertainer in the world. Highlighting both his life off-stage and some of the most iconic performances from his early solo career, the film gives audiences a front-row seat to Michael Jackson as never before. This is where his story begins.

verdict.reason: A cradle-to-grave Michael Jackson biopic focusing on his Jackson Five origins and early solo career. For die-hard MJ fans who need the big screen experience.

ratings.plot: Once seen, never forgotten. Pauline Kael called EYES WITHOUT A FACE "the most elegant horror movie ever made" and she ain't foolin'. This lyrical masterpiece is an incredible amalgam of mad science and fairy-tale that envelopes you like an ethereal fog. A creepy doctor is responsible for horribly disfiguring the face of his daughter (played by ethereal Edith Scob), leaving her in a featureless white mask. Best solution: kidnap young women, remove their faces and graft them onto his beloved child. Director Georges Franju is one of the fathers of the French Cinematheque and a true giant of French film history. He's also a big nerd who's into all sorts of genre fun. The poetic artistry that he brings to his pulp fantastique is art of the highest caliber.

Imagine that a local cable station was invaded by otherdimensional creatures at the stroke of midnight. That's Video Vortex. Each month, this series presents a mystery transmission that's culled from the outer limits of home video history – from essential underground films to surreal docs, from tokusatsu battle-parties to outsider experiments. So warm up the cathode tubes, dim the lights, and let your senses intermingle with the glow.

In the first time of the Tirology, small-tiem gangster Ugo Piazza has just been released from prison. He tries to convince the police, the mafia, and his one-time associate Rocco, a sadistic hoodlum who enjoys sick violence and torture that he wants to go straight, but everyone believes that he has $300,000 of stolen money hidden somewhere. CALIBER 9's stylized violence, fast-paced action sequence, tight editing and plot twists prefigure the work of Quentin Tarantino and John Woo. The film also features a notable score by Luis Bacalov and the stunning Barbara Bouchet as Ugo's go-go dancing girlfriend. (Kino Lorber)

"ok it's exploitative and moderately offensive but when was the last time you saw a movie with a female heroine who is free to be a complete asshole?" - sydney, Letterboxd In the roller derby classic UNHOLY ROLLERS, Claudia Jennings turns in what a very impressed Roger Ebert called "the hardest, most vicious female performance in a long time." She plays the kind of Barbara Stanwyck, Joan Blondell gum-chewing, working-class heroine that Hollywood excelled at depicting before the Production Code stepped in and made screen women all soft and domesticated. Here Jennings rises from a dreary life working in a cat-food factory to become a roller-derby queen who skates to win, no matter what the script says. She drinks cheap beer, curses like a sailor, and generally doesn't give a fuck. Sadly, Jennings, who died in a car crash in 1979, didn't get any other parts this good. Written and directed by Vernon Zimmerman, who later made the excellent FADE TO BLACK, and provided the screenplay for TEEN WITCH. Edited by Martin Scorsese. (Lars Nilsen)

Presented in Japanese with English subtitles Eiji (Kôichi Imaizumi) is a professional cameraman who spends his nights shooting covert footage of sexual acts around the city for the shadowy adult video studio that employs him. One night while out shooting, he watches a young woman named Saki (Rei Takagi) slash the eyes of her john with a boxcutter. Intrigued, he begins to follow her around, unaware that she—and her older sister, Maki (Kiyomi Itô)—are both connected to the same traumatic incident from his past. Filmmaker Hisayasu Satô updates the tropes of the standard “rape and revenge” drama for the 90s with his classic pink film TURTLE VISION, injecting the narrative with his usual obsessions with voyeurism and urban malaise. New 2K restoration by Vinegar Syndrome

It’s the bubbly and hilarious legal comedy that taught us to never judge a book by its fabulous look, and more importantly, how to bend and snap. Elle Woods (Reese Witherspoon), a fashionable sorority queen, is dumped by her dream boyfriend. She decides the way back into his heart is to follow him to law school, but she soon finds something far more rewarding than her bonehead ex as she exceeds all the stuck-up expectations. Witherspoon shines as the tough but always effervescent Elle in this bleached-out comedy about not only proving your worth but doing it for yourself. Just remember to keep that perm dry. Wouldn’t want to deactivate the ammonium thioglycolate.

movies.title: The Amazing Circus: The Last Act

ratings.plot: The viral internet sensation “The Amazing Digital Circus” comes to its long-awaited conclusion in a feature-length theatrical release! This “Last Act” combines episode 8 with the all-new, hour-long episode 9 for fans to see before anyone else in the world. With Caine gone and the circus dark, the cast are left with only the mistakes and traumas of their pasts to keep them company. As the prospect of eternity closes in around them, they discover the truth about the Digital Circus and its history. Will they come to terms with what they uncover, or will they make…the other choice? Also, presumably at some point, someone says something funny, because this ending can’t be THAT depressing, can it?
